jobs.title: Compliance Associate
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide
advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Lombard Odier Investment Managers (“LOIM”) is the asset management business of the Lombard Odier Group. In order to strengthen our Compliance Team, we are looking for a Luxembourg based:

Compliance Associate

YOUR ROLE

• Act as a key point of contact for compliance and AML (Anti-Money Laundering) matters within the organization.

• Support the implementation and ongoing enhancement of the compliance monitoring program, ensuring alignment with Luxembourg regulatory requirements (CSSF, AML/CFT, MiFID II, UCITS, AIFMD, etc.).

• Coordinate the migration of manual Compliance and AML processes (e.g., Excel-based) into automated Alteryx workflows and Tableau dashboards, driving digital transformation within the team.

• Collaborate with IT, business units, and other Compliance/AML team members to design and implement data-driven solutions that support decision-making and business tracking.

• Conduct compliance risk assessments, gap analyses, and thematic reviews.

• Monitor regulatory developments and assess their impact on business activities; ensure timely communication and implementation of new requirements.

• Draft, review, and update compliance policies, procedures, and internal controls.

• Support and coordinate regulatory reporting, internal/external audits, and respond to regulatory inquiries.

YOUR PROFILE

• University degree in Law, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field; additional compliance certifications (e.g., CAMS, ICA, or equivalent) are a strong asset.

• Relevant experience (up to 3 years) in a compliance, AML, or risk management role within the asset management or financial services industry, ideally in Luxembourg.

• Knowledge of Luxembourg and EU regulatory frameworks (CSSF, AML/CFT, MiFID II, UCITS, AIFMD, FATCA/CRS, etc.).

• Experience with Business Intelligence platforms (Tableau, Alteryx) and a strong interest in process automation and data analytics.

• Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills; ability to manage mu

jobs.title: Juriste
Site Reliability Engineer (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Site Reliability Engineer (80-100%)

As part of the modernization of our IT platform, we are looking for a Platform-oriented Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) who can support us in increasing our maturity around containers and the related ecosystem.

YOUR ROLE

• Ensure the reliability, scalability, and performance of the OpenShift platform and its ecosystem (Kong, Kafka, Vault).

• Manage the platform lifecycle: provisioning, updates, patching, and capacity planning.

• Implement and maintain observability solutions (metrics, logs, tracing) using Prometheus, Grafana, and associated tools.

• Establish governance for observability and operability of applications hosted on the platform.

• Handle incident response and perform root cause analysis for platform-related issues.

• Collaborate with security teams to apply best practices and ensure compliance in containerized environments.

• Contribute to the continuous improvement of the platform architecture and operational processes.

• Internal contacts : Databases, Systems, Telecom, Citrix, Development, Service Management (Incidents and Problems), Integration.

YOUR PROFILE

• Higher education in Computer Science (master’s degree or equivalent).

• 5 to 10 years of experience in a similar role

• Excellent command of OpenShift and/or Kubernetes administration

• Excellent command of monitoring and alerting systems (Prometheus, Grafana) and overall operational maintenance

• Excellent command of Linux (RHEL/CentOS)

• Excellent command of scripting (Shell, Python)

• Knowledge networking, security, and RBAC concepts in containerized environments

• Knowledge of high availability and disaster recovery strategies

• Responsible, rigorous, versatile, flexible, and with strong interpersonal skills. Team player, continuous improvement mindset, proactive

• Fluent in French and English

• Resident in Switzerland or willing to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.title: Business Risk Manager (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Risk Manager (80-100%) - LO Patrimonia

LO Patrimonia is an entity of Lombard Odier Group providing wealth planning and fiduciary services. With a team of forty experts whose skills cover Switzerland, the main European jurisdictions and other regions of the world, LO Patrimonia serves clients of the Lombard Odier Group exclusively. To cope with a growing and challenging regulatory environment, we are looking for a Risk and Control Officer.
The person will maintain an operational risk control plan and perform controls to ensure LO Patrimonia complies with its regulatory obligations and mitigates risks in accordance with Lombard Odier Group’s requirements. This includes the improvement of operational efficiency of tools and processes.
The person will work closely with LO Patrimonia’s management and legal/compliance responsible person as well as collaborate with other departments of Lombard Odier Group in the field of risk, operational and technology management.

YOUR MISSION

• Maintain LO Patrimonia’s risk control environment including governance, risk committee and reportings in accordance with Group Risk’s requirements

• Monitor activities and perform and/or consolidate controls for the risk committee

• Perform controls to monitor LO Patrimonia’s compliancy with regulatory obligations

• Act as data owner

• Act as Business Risk Manager and support LO Patrimonia’s management to comply with Lombard Odier Group Risk’ requirements

• Coordinate Internal Audit requests with relevant stakeholders and implement recommendations

• Manage the delivery of multiple projects requested by LO Patrimonia’s management or legal/compliance responsible person from initial analysis to implementation

• Collaborate to wider legal and regulatory projects initiated from other departments of the Lombard Odier Group and with the assistance of the department in charge of operations and organization

• Lead a variety of projects, ranging from electronic signature implementation to document management (GED) initiatives

YOUR PROFILE

• Minimum 5 years of relevant work experience in banking or fiduciary environment in a similar role or equivalent

• Good knowledge of regulatory requirements of Swiss Law, specifically FINMA’s circulars on AML and risk topics, corporate law, AMLA, FADP and cross-border rules (soft law)

• Good experience in the fields of compliance and risk researches and controls

• Knowledge of SQL language and overall understanding of IT hardware and software in order to be able to exchange with IT persons and popularize for non IT persons

• Proficient level with Microsoft office tools, especially Excel, Powerpoint, Adobe acrobat and ability to self-learn other applications

• Versatile, analytical, long term oriented, good interpersonal skills, willing to learn, tenacious, team player and communicator

• Fluent in French and English

• Resident in Switzerland

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at wh

jobs.title: Senior Compliance Assurance Officer (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Senior Compliance Assurance Officer (80-100%)

Group Compliance Assurance is a dedicated team within Lombard Odier's Compliance department. The team based in Switzerland is responsible for implementing the Group Compliance Assurance Plan for our Swiss and foreign entities. The scope of the team's activities is in line with the mandate of the Compliance Department, focuses primarily on risks related to financial crime / sanctions and materially contributes to our Compliance Consolidated Supervision framework. The team's activities embed a forward-looking approach, with a view to translate the results of risk-based assurance activities into measurable improvements in the Compliance control environment.

YOUR ROLE

Based in Geneva and reporting to the Head of Group Compliance Assurance, you will assess (i) the design and operating effectiveness of relevant controls; (ii) compliance with applicable laws and regulations and adherence to our Compliance standards and internal policies for the Group Lombard Odier as follows:

• Develop the testing and review plans, including maintaining an inventory of test scripts.

• Perform and/or supervise testing and/or review activities

• Evaluate test and review results and communicate preliminary findings to stakeholders

• Document findings and their disposition as issues and actions in consultation with issue owners.

• Finalise and distribute reports to stakeholders.

• Monitor the completion of issues and actions through to issue closure for management reporting.

• Prepare and report progress status metrics as required

• Coordinate compliance assurance activities with other second line of defence units and Group Internal Audit

• Provide advice and guidance to the front office on actions to improve compliance in relation to the results of testing and reviews

• Propose and contribute to projects to continuously improve the control environment for compliance-related risks and other relevant cross-functional initiatives

• In accordance with Lombard Odier's professional standards, you will be expected to comply with all applicable legal, regulatory and internal compliance requirements, including but not limited to the Group Code of Conduct and Compliance Policies and Procedures. You will also be expected to maintain all professional certifications, as applicable, to be fully qualified to perform your role, including completing all mandatory training required to obtain and maintain such certifications.

YOUR PROFILE

Required qualifications/education

• Degree in law, economics, finance or a related field

• Additional professional certification in compliance an advantage
Experience/knowledge required

• Solid understanding of regulatory environments, particularly of the Swiss framework and the organization of consolidated supervision

• At least 10 years of relevant experience in financial services in the public or private sector, with particular exposure to private banking

• Experience in a compliance or audit ro

jobs.title: Senior Executive Assistant (70-100%)
Junior Change Manager (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

We are looking for a Junior Change Manager to assist the Head of PMO & Communication in supporting strategic initiatives and operational excellence across the CP COO Office.

YOUR ROLE

Change Management & Adoption

• Analyze change impacts and stakeholder readiness related to new tools, processes, or organizational changes.

• Define and execute change management plans (communication, training, engagement, adoption metrics).

• Act as a key liaison between business teams, IT, and project teams to ensure alignment and smooth change deployment.

• Identify resistance to change and proactively propose mitigation actions.

Communication & Content Management

• Contribute to the definition of editorial calendars for internal communication related to transformation initiatives.

• Ensure consistency of tone, branding, and messaging across all communication channels.

• Monitor and analyze communication effectiveness (open rates, engagement, feedback) and adjust strategies accordingly.

• Coordinate mass mailing campaigns and internal communications (newsletters, updates, invitations), ensuring timely delivery, consistency of messaging, and high quality proofreading.

Training & governance

• Design training strategies adapted to different user profiles and maturity levels.

• Coordinate the creation and maintenance of training materials and knowledge repositories.

• Produce AI‑generated training videos (e.g. tutorials, demos, explainers) to support scalable, engaging, and on‑demand learning.

• Track change-related KPIs (adoption rate, satisfaction, training completion, communication reach).

• Contribute to change management standards, templates, and best practices.

YOUR PROFILE

• University degree in Finance, Business Management, Engineering or related fields, or evidence of comparable academic / work achievement.

• 2-4 years relevant experience in Consulting in a PMO or Project Management Role with experience in financial services

• Able to prioritize in a fast moving, high pressure, constantly changing environment,

• Adaptability and resilience in a fast-paced, high-stakes banking environment.

• Strong analytical skills, the ability to summarize complex topics and convey key messages using proficiently MS Office tools such as PowerPoint, Excel

• Fluent in English and French; excellent communication (written and verbal), networking, influencing and presentation skills

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professi

jobs.title: Private Assets Investment Analyst
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Lombard Odier Investment Managers (“LOIM”) is the asset management business of the Lombard Odier Group. In order to strengthen our Private Assets department, we are looking for a Geneva based:

Investment Analyst- Private Assets

As an Investment Analyst, you will work closely with Investment Managers and broader team members across all stages of the private equity investment cycle, contributing to the sourcing, assessment, and monitoring of private asset opportunities

YOUR ROLE

• Maintain and update the private assets fund opportunity pipeline

• Analyse new private equity fund investments, co‑investments/directs, industry trends, and market dynamics

• Perform detailed due diligence on investment opportunities, including qualitative and quantitative analysis

• Prepare investment memoranda, due diligence documentation, and internal committee materials

• Monitor and report on existing fund investments, providing in‑depth performance insights

• Support the preparation of marketing materials for LOIM private equity strategies and products

• Develop sales pitches, presentations, and other materials for internal and external stakeholders

YOUR PROFILE

• Master’s degree, ideally in business, economics, finance, or engineering

• Prior professional experience in Private Equity, Corporate Finance, or Investment Banking is essential

• Native-level proficiency in English; professional working proficiency in French

• Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with excellent attention to detail

• Proactive, innovative, and results‑driven mindset

• Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple priorities

• Outstanding interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to work both independently and collaboratively

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

The Group Tax Department contributes to the value creation of the Group by controlling its tax. Joining the Group Tax Department as a Tax Intern in our Geneva headquarters, you will :

• Gain understanding of the daily work and organization of a Corporate Tax Affairs Department at the head office of a leading multinational group

• Obtain valuable professional experience whilst supporting the members of the Group Tax Department

YOUR ROLE

As an intern within our Group Tax Department, you will have the opportunity to participate in current projects and new initiatives. The role mainly consists in assisting all the team members in dealing with Swiss and International direct and indirect tax subjects.

Working closely with the entire tax team you may have the opportunity to perform the following tasks:

• Contribute actively to the internal domestic and international tax watch

• Conduct tax studies and legal researches to address concerns raised by the various stakeholders

• Participate in updating the transfer pricing documentation and tax disclosure/compliance obligations

• Assist in the preparation of tax computations and compliance work

• Support the team on indirect tax projects

• Assist when dealing with tax administrations’ requests

• Ad-hoc project work

YOUR PROFILE

• Strong interest for corporate taxation

• Master’s degree obtained (or in progress) in tax law, accounting or finance

• Fluent in French and English is necessary

• Good knowledge of Microsoft office suite (especially Excel and Word)

• Ambitious, enthusiastic, with a team oriented spirit and curious to learn through a first experience within a leading multinational group. You enjoy working closely and interact with colleagues within an international environment

• Available per July or September 2026

• Domicile in Switzerland

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.title: Développeur Full-stack IA/ML & Reporting Designer
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 225 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Développeur Full-stack IA/ML & Reporting Designer

Rejoignez le département Technology & Operations de Lombard Odier au sein de l'équipe Content and Communications, responsable de la plateforme de gestion de contenu et de la production/distribution des documents clients et partenaires.

Nous recherchons un développeur Full-stack IA/ML et Reporting Designer afin de participer au développement de notre nouvelle plateforme technologique bancaire qui est également mise à disposition d’autres banques privées suisses et européennes.

Intégré(e) aux équipes produits, vous apporterez une expertise à l’intersection des LLM et de l’ingénierie logicielle appliquées à la finance : recherche documentaire augmentée (RAG), orchestration et optimisation d’inférence (on‑prem/cloud), intégration des modèles dans les parcours front‑office, et automatisation du reporting en production. Vous industrialiserez la génération et la personnalisation des documents via la suite Quadient Inspire (templates, composants, workflows et publication), en garantissant la qualité, la traçabilité et l’auditabilité de bout en bout. Vous évoluerez au sein d’une organisation agile, proche des utilisateurs finaux et du métier, au contact de technologies modernes et éprouvées.

VOTRE MISSION

• Contribuer aux développements de nos applications de gestion de contenu et de la production/distribution des documents.

• Comprendre les besoins des utilisateurs, les problèmes à résoudre ainsi que les contraintes d’ordre technique.

• Proposer et implémenter des solutions technologiques adaptées et tenant compte des aspects précités.

VOTRE PROFIL

• Master en informatique avec spécialisation en IA, Machine Learning et Data analytics suivi dans l'une des universités polytechnique suisse.

• Première expérience professionnelle ou stage significatif dans le développement applicatif, le reporting ou les outils documentaires.

• Computer Vision : Maîtrise des LLM/VLM appliqués aux documents (RAG, extraction visuelle, compréhension de mise en page).

• Expérience en détection d’anomalies documentaires (contrôles visuels, cohérence sémantique, règles métier).

• Maîtrise des langages de programmation Java et Python

• Expérience en LLM engineering : fine‑tuning, quantization, RAG (LangChain/LangGraph), et orchestration (LlamaIndex).

• Connaissance du framework de développement d’application web Angular

• Expérience conteneurs (Dockers / Kubernetes) et DevOps

• Expérience pratique des méthodologies Agile, Scrum est un plus

• Maîtrise courante du français et de l’anglais

• Vous avez une excellente communication à l’écrit comme à l’oral

• Vous avez un bon esprit d’analyse et une bonne capacité d'abstraction afin d’identifier et expliciter le problème à résoudre avant de penser aux solutions

• Vous êtes capable de donner du feedback constructif et prêt à en recevoir

• Vous faites preuve d’une grande autonomie

• Domicilié en Suisse ou disposé à vous y établir

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core valu

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Développeur Full-stack IA/ML & Reporting Designer

Rejoignez le département Technology & Operations de Lombard Odier au sein de l'équipe Content and Communications, responsable de la plateforme de gestion de contenu et de la production/distribution des documents clients et partenaires.

Nous recherchons un développeur Full-stack IA/ML et Reporting Designer afin de participer au développement de notre nouvelle plateforme technologique bancaire qui est également mise à disposition d’autres banques privées suisses et européennes.

Intégré(e) aux équipes produits, vous apporterez une expertise à l’intersection des LLM et de l’ingénierie logicielle appliquées à la finance : recherche documentaire augmentée (RAG), orchestration et optimisation d’inférence (on‑prem/cloud), intégration des modèles dans les parcours front‑office, et automatisation du reporting en production. Vous industrialiserez la génération et la personnalisation des documents via la suite Quadient Inspire (templates, composants, workflows et publication), en garantissant la qualité, la traçabilité et l’auditabilité de bout en bout. Vous évoluerez au sein d’une organisation agile, proche des utilisateurs finaux et du métier, au contact de technologies modernes et éprouvées.

VOTRE MISSION

• Contribuer aux développements de nos applications de gestion de contenu et de la production/distribution des documents.

• Comprendre les besoins des utilisateurs, les problèmes à résoudre ainsi que les contraintes d’ordre technique.

• Proposer et implémenter des solutions technologiques adaptées et tenant compte des aspects précités.

VOTRE PROFIL

• Master en informatique avec spécialisation en IA, Machine Learning et Data analytics suivi dans l'une des universités polytechnique suisse.

• Première expérience professionnelle ou stage significatif dans le développement applicatif, le reporting ou les outils documentaires.

• Computer Vision : Maîtrise des LLM/VLM appliqués aux documents (RAG, extraction visuelle, compréhension de mise en page).

• Expérience en détection d’anomalies documentaires (contrôles visuels, cohérence sémantique, règles métier).

• Maîtrise des langages de programmation Java et Python

• Expérience en LLM engineering : fine‑tuning, quantization, RAG (LangChain/LangGraph), et orchestration (LlamaIndex).

• Connaissance du framework de développement d’application web Angular

• Expérience conteneurs (Dockers / Kubernetes) et DevOps

• Expérience pratique des méthodologies Agile, Scrum est un plus

• Maîtrise courante du français et de l’anglais

• Vous avez une excellente communication à l’écrit comme à l’oral

• Vous avez un bon esprit d’analyse et une bonne capacité d'abstraction afin d’identifier et expliciter le problème à résoudre avant de penser aux solutions

• Vous êtes capable de donner du feedback constructif et prêt à en recevoir

• Vous faites preuve d’une grande autonomie

• Domicilié en Suisse ou disposé à vous y établir

jobs.title: Senior Accountant
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Senior Accountant (100%)

Vous serez amené(e) à prendre en charge différentes activités en lien avec des clients de la Banque Lombard Odier, à entretenir un contact permanent avec ces derniers et à collaborer avec les autres services de LO Patrimonia et de la Banque.

VOTRE MISSION :

• Tenue des comptabilités de sociétés (principalement de détentions d’actifs), fondations (principalement caritatives)

• Accompagnement et suivi régulier d’un client en particulier

• Passation d’écritures comptables (y compris le bouclement)

• Etablissement des documents de reporting nécessaires

• Suivi administratif lié aux tâches de comptabilité

• Contacts avec les réviseurs

• Participation à la gestion et au développement des outils informatiques comptables

VOTRE PROFIL :

• Très bonnes connaissances comptables (règles comptables suisses et internationales-IFRS).

• Expérience professionnelle de minimum 10 années dans le domaine bancaire et/ou fiduciaire.

• Connaissance du fonctionnement des caisses de pension un atout.

• A l’aise avec des systèmes informatiques multiples, maîtrisant notamment Excel, fonctions et macros avancées.

• Notions de gestion des salaires un atout.

• Très bonnes connaissances de l'anglais.

• Esprit d’équipe, autonome, flexible, précision et sens du détail.

• Sens des responsabilités, entregent et bonne capacité relationnelle.

• Intérêt et facilité pour les contacts avec les différents intervenants (conseillers juridiques et fiscaux, gérants des entités, banquiers).

• Domicile en Suisse.

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.title: Compensation and Benefits Specialist (9-month contract)
Head of IT Security Operations & Controls (80-100%)

titleByLocale.en: Head of IT Security Operations & Controls (80-100%)

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Head of IT Security Operations & Controls (80-100%)

The CISO organization protects Lombard Odier and our Clients against the risk of data theft and business disruption due to cyber-attacks or malicious insiders. The team is composed of approx. 15 talented team members that cover all aspects of security for the Group.

Reporting to our Chief Information Security Officer, you will lead the bank’s Operational Security function - owning our SoC (analysts and engineering), incident response, and the effectiveness of our frontline security controls. Your mission is to maximize the safe use of our digital assets, protect customers information, and keep the business fast, compliant, and resilient.

YOUR ROLE

• Manage a team of approx. 4 individuals: hiring, coaching, and creating an environment where people do the best work of their careers.

• SoC leadership & incident response: Run day‑to‑day SoC operations, elevate detection & response maturity, and lead major incident command calmly under pressure. Ensure investigations are rigorous, evidence‑based, and drive tactical fixes as well as strategic improvements.

• Strategy & modernization: Define and execute the SoC strategy for the next 2–3 years, including pragmatic adoption of cloud and AI‑assisted threat detection, triage, and automation (SOAR).

• Security control operations: Own the operating effectiveness of key controls (e.g., vulnerability management, baseline security, DLP). Ensure continuous control monitoring, coverage metrics, and clear KRIs/KPIs that matter to the business.

• Establish, own, and execute the bank’s penetration testing, red team, and purple team roadmap -coordinating internal capabilities and external partners to simulate realistic adversary behaviors and ensure continuous improvement of detection, response, and control effectiveness.

• Oversee the operational workflow of the security team, including triaging incoming support tickets, security requests, and operational tasks. Ensure efficient dispatching and prioritization of work across the CISO organization and maintain clear service‑level expectations with internal stakeholders.

• Enterprise & engineering integration: Partner closely with Architecture, Engineering, Infrastructure, IT Ops, and Developers to land controls and patterns that scale.

• Resilience & recovery: Strengthen incident readiness, tabletop exercises, and post‑incident “close‑call” learning to boost resilience and reduce repeat issues.

• Stakeholder trust: Engage customers, auditors, and internal leaders; translate risk into clear business context; advocate for secure‑by‑default choices.

YOUR PROFILE

• 10+ years in cybersecurity with direct leadership of SoC and Incident Response functions, including people leadership (hiring, coaching, performance).

• Demonstrated success running security operations in complex environments (on‑premise and cloud)

• Strong systems thinking; you connect detections, controls, processes, and behaviors into a coherent

Reporting to our Chief Information Security Officer, you will lead the bank’s Operational Security function - owning our SoC (analysts and engineering), incident response, and the effectiveness of our frontline security controls. Your mission is to maximize the safe use of our digital assets, protect customers information, and keep the business fast, compliant, and resilient.

YOUR ROLE

• Manage a team of approx. 4 individuals: hiring, coaching, and creating an environment where people do the best work of their careers.

• SoC leadership & incident response: Run day‑to‑day SoC operations, elevate detection & response maturity, and lead major incident command calmly under pressure. Ensure investigations are rigorous, evidence‑based, and drive tactical fixes as well as strategic improvements.

• Strategy & modernization: Define and execute the SoC strategy for the next 2–3 years, including pragmatic adoption of cloud and AI‑assisted threat detection, triage, and automation (SOAR).

• Security control operations: Own the operating effectiveness of key controls (e.g., vulnerability management, baseline security, DLP). Ensure continuous control monitoring, coverage metrics, and clear KRIs/KPIs that matter to the business.

• Establish, own, and execute the bank’s penetration testing, red team, and purple team roadmap -coordinating internal capabilities and external partners to simulate realistic adversary behaviors and ensure continuous improvement of detection, response, and control effectiveness.

• Oversee the operational workflow of the security team, including triaging incoming support tickets, security requests, and operational tasks. Ensure efficient dispatching and prioritization of work across the CISO organization and maintain clear service‑level expectations with internal stakeholders.

• Enterprise & engineering integration: Partner closely with Architecture, Engineering, Infrastructure, IT Ops, and Developers to land controls and patterns that scale.

• Resilience & recovery: Strengthen incident readiness, tabletop exercises, and post‑incident “close‑call” learning to boost resilience and reduce repeat issues.

• Stakeholder trust: Engage customers, auditors, and internal leaders; translate risk into clear business context; advocate for secure‑by‑default choices.

YOUR PROFILE

• 10+ years in cybersecurity with direct leadership of SoC and Incident Response functions, including people leadership (hiring, coaching, performance).

• Demonstrated success running security operations in complex environments (on‑premise and cloud)

• Strong systems thinking; you connect detections, controls, processes, and behaviors into a coherent

jobs.title: Junior HRIS Environment & Support Specialist (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Junior HRIS Environment & Support Specialist (80-100%)

We are seeking a motivated Junior HRIS Environment & Support Specialist to join our HR Digital Transformation team. This role is ideal for someone with a strong IT foundation and a passion for supporting and improving HR systems. You will be trained in Workday and other technologies, and play a key role in maintaining and enhancing our HR digital ecosystem.
You’ll be part of a dynamic and collaborative team driving digital transformation in HR. The team vision is to enhance collaborators’ experience, achieve greater efficiency, and minimize risks.

YOUR ROLE

• Monitor and maintain the HR digital ecosystem to ensure stability and performance.

• Serve as the main point of contact for HRIS support and troubleshooting.

• Execute operational tasks such as monthly reporting and data quality checks.

• Implement solutions to reduce manual workload across HR teams.

• Ensure the run and maintenance of HR IT systems, including Workday and related tools.

• Maintain and monitor automated jobs and reports to ensure timely and accurate delivery.

• Manage and optimize HR-related data flows and database structures.

• Document HRIS procedures, workflows, and technical configurations to ensure knowledge sharing and continuity.

• Stay informed on emerging technologies, including AI, and contribute to the team’s technological watch.

YOUR PROFILE

• Master’s degree or Engineering degree in Information Technology or related field.

• More than 2 years of professional experience in IT or HRIS support.

• Excellent command of scripting (CMD batches) and Office 365 (particularly Excel)

• Good command of Windows Server administration

• Strong Interest in technological innovation and awareness of AI trends

• Strong problem-solving and analytical skills

• Proactive, adaptable, and service-oriented mindset

• Comfortable with change and continuous learning

• Autonomous and well-organized

• Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities

• Fluent in French and a good working level of English

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

TECHNICAL ENVIRONMENT

• Windows Servers Administration basics

• Windows scripting (CMD batch)

• Microsoft Office Suite (advanced in Excel)

• Transact-SQL queries

• Advanced C# / .NET development skills

• Microsoft Visual Studio

• Version control tools (e.g. Git, SVN)

• Workday administration and integrations

• Microsoft SSIS (SQL Server Integration Services)

• Microsoft SSRS (SQL Server Reporting Services)

• Azure DevOps

• Agile methodologies

• PHP

• SQL Server Database Management

• Atlassian tools (Jira, Confluence)

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we

jobs.title: Product Owner – Risk & Compliance (CRM Platform)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Product Owner – Risk & Compliance (CRM Platform)

Lombard Odier Group operates its own proprietary banking technology platform used by the Group and provided to other private banks and financial institutions through a BPO business model. The Technology & Operations (T&O) department maintains and develops the platform through the delivery of solutions for the Bank’s business divisions.

Within Lombard Odier’s Development department, we seek to strengthen our teams with an experienced Product Owner dedicated to the Risk & Compliance domain within our Customer Relationship Management (CRM) product. Our bankers’ CRM—primarily developed in-house—is fully integrated within our Wealth Management application suite and is a key differentiator of our technology offering. Given the central role of client lifecycle management and first-line controls in Private Banking, we aim to bring our CRM’s Risk & Compliance capabilities to the next level.

As a Product Owner for Risk & Compliance within the CRM platform, you will be part of the Wealth Management software development team and report to the CRM Product Manager. The team, working in an agile framework, brings together all “front-office” products serving bankers, relationship managers, clients, and key control functions. In your day-to-day work, you will collaborate with peer product owners, product managers, project managers, business analysts, developers, and—crucially—business stakeholders across Private Clients, Group Compliance & Risk, Tax, and Regulatory Reporting.

YOUR ROLE

• Define the product vision and roadmap for Risk & Compliance in CRM

• Own the end-to-end design and evolution of AML/KYC risk scoring within CRM

• Implement and streamline regulatory suitability & appropriateness processes

• Act as the primary interface between business stakeholders and the product team, ensuring clear understanding, timely feedback loops, and resolution of emerging needs and issues.

• Perform continuous market and regulatory watch on compliance tooling and operating practices (e.g., KYC/CLM, suitability, AML risk engines) to benchmark and anticipate changes.

• Proactively work with our 3rd party suppliers to drive value, establishing a solid and sound relationship.

• Lead potential RFI/RFP processes

• Foster a positive product team dynamic while maximizing ROI of product features as well as end users’ satisfaction.

• Maintain & prioritize the backlog by including changes and project demands while ensuring product maintenance to guarantee stability and sustainability over the long term.

• Set and communicate work priorities, updates and issues that emerge during the development process.

• Create sprint goals, actionable epics and user stories based on the requirements defined for each feature (function) in collaboration with the product team.

• Participate in project estimation costs based on business requirements.

• Ensure high product quality is met by overseeing test strategy definition, execution, and

descriptionByLocale.de:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Product Owner – Risk & Compliance (CRM Platform)

Lombard Odier Group operates its own proprietary banking technology platform used by the Group and provided to other private banks and financial institutions through a BPO business model. The Technology & Operations (T&O) department maintains and develops the platform through the delivery of solutions for the Bank’s business divisions.

Within Lombard Odier’s Development department, we seek to strengthen our teams with an experienced Product Owner dedicated to the Risk & Compliance domain within our Customer Relationship Management (CRM) product. Our bankers’ CRM—primarily developed in-house—is fully integrated within our Wealth Management application suite and is a key differentiator of our technology offering. Given the central role of client lifecycle management and first-line controls in Private Banking, we aim to bring our CRM’s Risk & Compliance capabilities to the next level.

As a Product Owner for Risk & Compliance within the CRM platform, you will be part of the Wealth Management software development team and report to the CRM Product Manager. The team, working in an agile framework, brings together all “front-office” products serving bankers, relationship managers, clients, and key control functions. In your day-to-day work, you will collaborate with peer product owners, product managers, project managers, business analysts, developers, and—crucially—business stakeholders across Private Clients, Group Compliance & Risk, Tax, and Regulatory Reporting.

YOUR ROLE

• Define the product vision and roadmap for Risk & Compliance in CRM

• Own the end-to-end design and evolution of AML/KYC risk scoring within CRM

• Implement and streamline regulatory suitability & appropriateness processes

• Act as the primary interface between business stakeholders and the product team, ensuring clear understanding, timely feedback loops, and resolution of emerging needs and issues.

• Perform continuous market and regulatory watch on compliance tooling and operating practices (e.g., KYC/CLM, suitability, AML risk engines) to benchmark and anticipate changes.

• Proactively work with our 3rd party suppliers to drive value, establishing a solid and sound relationship.

• Lead potential RFI/RFP processes

• Foster a positive product team dynamic while maximizing ROI of product features as well as end users’ satisfaction.

• Maintain & prioritize the backlog by including changes and project demands while ensuring product maintenance to guarantee stability and sustainability over the long term.

• Set and communicate work priorities, updates and issues that emerge during the development process.

• Create sprint goals, actionable epics and user stories based on the requirements defined for each feature (function) in collaboration with the product team.

• Participate in project estimation costs based on business requirements.

• Ensure high product quality is met by overseeing test strategy definition, execution, and

jobs.title: Application Support Specialist - Front Office
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Application Support Specialist - Front Office

We are looking for an Intern within the IT Operations department to provide application support for Front tools (Wealth Management) to the client-facing and operational units of Lombard Odier (Europe) S.A., as well as to our external partners.

YOUR ROLE

• Provide functional support for Wealth Management applications

• Identify, resolve, and troubleshoot all issues raised within the team

• Ensure maximum availability of applications and deliver quick solutions to Front teams’ queries

• Participate in quality checks and drafting of new application releases

• Train users on applications and assist our partners during UAT

YOUR PROFILE

• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science or equivalent

• Strong interest in the banking sector

• Strong analytical and teamwork skills with a keen interest in client interaction

• Sense of responsibility

• Ability to work independently with minimal supervision

• Proficiency in French and English

• Resident in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.title: Business Application Support Specialist – Finance (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Application Support Specialist – Finance (80-100%)

A Business Application Support Specialist plays a crucial role in ensuring the stability, availability, and optimal performance of banking applications. This role involves providing technical support, troubleshooting issues, and collaborating with various teams to maintain a reliable banking application environment. He is responsible for providing to the employees and to customers of Lombard Odier group with functional support applications in Finance domain. This support team’s main mission is the satisfaction and trust of colleagues and clients.

YOUR ROLE

• Identify, resolve and troubleshoot all Functional Support issues for finance and accounting applications.

• Participating in quality controls and documentation of new applications releases notes.

• Monitor KPIs to ensure SLAs are met and the service is on time and reliable.

• Escalate major incidents to the incident management team to ensure timely resolution.

• Organize and manage regular meetings with Bank partners for escalated cases, monitor closely the backlog of tickets

• Analysis of balance sheet in SAP.

• Ensure a quality training for bank users.

• Participate in new application release testing.

• Work closely with development and production teams.

• Ensure regular contact with our Bank partners Finance team.

• Actively contribute to enhance the day-to-day process of the team by improvement proposals to the head of Application Support

YOUR PROFILE

• Master’s degree in IT or Finance

• +5 years of experience in Banking

• Excellent communication and overall soft skills, proactivity, sense of initiative and sound experience in working under stress on unplanned matters

• Excellent command of banking, finance related operations and understanding of accounting principles

• Good command of Microsoft Office tools, specifically Excel & PowerPoint

• Previous experience as a Business Analyst or as an Auditor in the banking and financial sector is an advantage

• SAP BI knowledge is an advantage

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

descriptionByLocale.fr:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Application Support Specialist – Finance (80-100%)

A Business Application Support Specialist plays a crucial role in ensuring the stability, availability, and optimal performance of banking applications. This role involves providing technical support, troubleshooting issues, and collaborating with various teams to maintain a reliable banking application environment. He is responsible for providing to the employees and to customers of Lombard Odier group with functional support applications in Finance domain. This support team’s main mission is the satisfaction and trust of colleagues and clients.

YOUR ROLE

• Identify, resolve and troubleshoot all Functional Support issues for finance and accounting applications.

• Participating in quality controls and documentation of new applications releases notes.

• Monitor KPIs to ensure SLAs are met and the service is on time and reliable.

• Escalate major incidents to the incident management team to ensure timely resolution.

• Organize and manage regular meetings with Bank partners for escalated cases, monitor closely the backlog of tickets

• Analysis of balance sheet in SAP.

• Ensure a quality training for bank users.

• Participate in new application release testing.

• Work closely with development and production teams.

• Ensure regular contact with our Bank partners Finance team.

• Actively contribute to enhance the day-to-day process of the team by improvement proposals to the head of Application Support

YOUR PROFILE

• Master’s degree in IT or Finance

• +5 years of experience in Banking

• Excellent communication and overall soft skills, proactivity, sense of initiative and sound experience in working under stress on unplanned matters

• Excellent command of banking, finance related operations and understanding of accounting principles

• Good command of Microsoft Office tools, specifically Excel & PowerPoint

• Previous experience as a Business Analyst or as an Auditor in the banking and financial sector is an advantage

• SAP BI knowledge is an advantage

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Support Intern

Integrated to the Management Committee of Lombard Odier T&O Service (Europe) SA, as a Business Support Intern, you will gain hands-on experience in corporate governance and administrative support for executive leadership. This internship will provide exposure to legal frameworks, risk management, and decision-making processes within a corporate environment. Working closely with experienced professionals, you will develop essential skills in organization, communication, and confidentiality.

YOUR ROLE
- Coordination and preparation of presentation materials for the Management Committee (Townhall, ExCo, etc)
- Drafting & circulation of meeting minutes
- Preparation of Reporting such as Reg Reporting
- Management of common mailbox,
- Coordination of internal (sport committee, etc) & external events
- Procedures/Policies Management :

• Coordination with their respective managers regarding deadlines for updating procedures

• Coordination of the formal validation process

• · Management of publications on the Intranet and communication with LOT&OS Employees

YOUR PROFILE
As the sole person in this role, you will benefit from significant autonomy and the following qualities will be expected :
- Strong organizational and anticipation skills,
- Adaptibility,
- Strong interpersonal and diplomatic skills,
- High level of confidentiality,
- Strong writing skills in French and English
- Very good command of Words and Office package.

NB: Please be aware that in accordance with the current law and job requirements, the selected candidate will be requested to provide a criminal record (section 3).

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.title: Account und Relationship Manager - Global Custody und Private Label Funds Kunden (80-100%)

titleByLocale.de: Account und Relationship Manager - Global Custody und Private Label Funds Kunden (80-100%)
titleByLocale.en: Account und Relationship Manager - Global Custody und Private Label Funds Kunden (80-100%)
titleByLocale.it: Account e Relationship Manager - Global Custody e Private Label Funds Kunden (80-100%)
titleByLocale.fr: Account und Relationship Manager - Global Custody und Private Label Funds Kunden (80-100%)

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Account und Relationship Manager - Global Custody und Private Label Funds Kunden (80-100%)

Als Teil eines kleinen, dynamischen und kundenorientierten Teams sind Sie verantwortlich für die umfassende Betreuung von strategisch wichtigen institutionellen Kunden in der Deutschschweiz.

Stellenbeschreibung:

• Umfassende Betreuung der Global Custody- und Private Label Funds Kunden (inkl. Trouble Shooting) in folgenden Bereichen: Depotbankdienstleistungen, Fondsbuchhaltung, Transfer Agent Aktivitäten, Bankinfrastruktur-Dienstleistungen, Business Process Outsourcing usw.

• Schnittstelle zwischen Kunden und diversen internen Stellen in Zürich, Genf und Luxembourg (Fondsleitung, Fondsbuchhaltung, Wertschriften-Operations, IT, Legal, Tax etc.) sowie Drittparteien (Vermögensverwalter, Revisoren, Consultants etc.)

• Eigenständige Führung von vielseitigen Kundenprojekten und Business Analyst Arbeiten (z.B. Lancierung von Anlagefonds, Offertstellung bei neuen Produkten, Service Reviews, technische Umsetzung von regulatorischen Anforderungen, Analyse von Kundenbedürfnissen etc.).

• Selbständige Vor- und Nachbearbeitung von Kundenmeetings (Lenkungsausschuss, operative Meetings etc.) und Führung / Teilnahme der Meetings

• Aufsetzen und Unterhalt von Verträgen und SLAs mit Kunden

• Erstellung von Kunden- und Gebührenabrechnungen und Ausführung von Gebührenchecks

• Eröffnung und Unterhalt der Kunden- und Kontenbeziehungen (Sicherstellung der korrekten Dokumentation, Aktualisierung der Kontodokumente und Verträge nach bankinternen Richtlinien etc.)

• Unterstützung bei der Ausführung von verschiedenen operativen Tätigkeiten wie Cash Management, Zahlungsverkehr, Abrechnung und Überwachung intern und extern gehandelter FX sowie Optionen- und Futures-Transaktionen. Koordination und Verbuchung von Transaktionen von nicht-deponierten Werten wie z.B. Private Equity, Infrastrukturanlagen, Immobilien etc.

• Mithilfe bei der Erstellung und Qualitätskontrolle von monatlichen und quartalsweisen kundenspezifischen Investmentreports (v.a. Fonds-Factsheets) für traditionelle und alternative Anlagen

Anforderungsprofil:

• Banklehre mit Berufsmaturität und abgeschlossene Weiterbildung (Bankwirtschafter/in HF, CFA /AZEK) oder Hochschulabschluss

• Mindestens fünf Jahre Berufserfahrung im Finanzsektor, idealerweise in der Betreuung von institutionellen Kunden

• Breite Fachkenntnisse im Bereich Asset Servicing (z. B. im Bereich Wertschriften, Börse, Back-& Middle-Office, Anlageberatung). Erfahrung im Kontakt mit externen Vermögensverwaltern und / oder Global Custody / Fonds-Kunden von Vorteil.

• Interesse am Fondsgeschäft, an operativen Abläufen (Prozessen) und Investmentthemen, verbunden mit Freude an Zahlen und regelmässigem Kontakt zu internen und externen Stellen

• Sehr gute MS Office Anwenderkenntnisse

• Kunden- und bedürfnisorientiert, hohes Qualitätsbewusstsein, genaue und selbstständige Arbeitsweise

• Teamfähige, engagierte und kommunikative Persönlichkeit mit hoher Soz
