• Breite Fachkenntnisse im Bereich Asset Servicing (z. B. im Bereich Wertschriften, Börse, Back-& Middle-Office, Anlageberatung). Erfahrung im Kontakt mit externen Vermögensverwaltern und / oder Global Custody / Fonds-Kunden von Vorteil.

• Interesse am Fondsgeschäft, an operativen Abläufen (Prozessen) und Investmentthemen, verbunden mit Freude an Zahlen und regelmässigem Kontakt zu internen und externen Stellen

• Sehr gute MS Office Anwenderkenntnisse

• Kunden- und bedürfnisorientiert, hohes Qualitätsbewusstsein, genaue und selbstständige Arbeitsweise

• Teamfähige, engagierte und kommunikative Persönlichkeit mit hoher Soz
descriptionByLocale.en:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Account und Relationship Manager - Global Custody und Private Label Funds Kunden (80-100%)

Als Teil eines kleinen, dynamischen und kundenorientierten Teams sind Sie verantwortlich für die umfassende Betreuung von strategisch wichtigen institutionellen Kunden in der Deutschschweiz.

Stellenbeschreibung:

• Umfassende Betreuung der Global Custody- und Private Label Funds Kunden (inkl. Trouble Shooting) in folgenden Bereichen: Depotbankdienstleistungen, Fondsbuchhaltung, Transfer Agent Aktivitäten, Bankinfrastruktur-Dienstleistungen, Business Process Outsourcing usw.

• Schnittstelle zwischen Kunden und diversen internen Stellen in Zürich, Genf und Luxembourg (Fondsleitung, Fondsbuchhaltung, Wertschriften-Operations, IT, Legal, Tax etc.) sowie Drittparteien (Vermögensverwalter, Revisoren, Consultants etc.)

• Eigenständige Führung von vielseitigen Kundenprojekten und Business Analyst Arbeiten (z.B. Lancierung von Anlagefonds, Offertstellung bei neuen Produkten, Service Reviews, technische Umsetzung von regulatorischen Anforderungen, Analyse von Kundenbedürfnissen etc.).

• Selbständige Vor- und Nachbearbeitung von Kundenmeetings (Lenkungsausschuss, operative Meetings etc.) und Führung / Teilnahme der Meetings

• Aufsetzen und Unterhalt von Verträgen und SLAs mit Kunden

• Erstellung von Kunden- und Gebührenabrechnungen und Ausführung von Gebührenchecks

• Eröffnung und Unterhalt der Kunden- und Kontenbeziehungen (Sicherstellung der korrekten Dokumentation, Aktualisierung der Kontodokumente und Verträge nach bankinternen Richtlinien etc.)

• Unterstützung bei der Ausführung von verschiedenen operativen Tätigkeiten wie Cash Management, Zahlungsverkehr, Abrechnung und Überwachung intern und extern gehandelter FX sowie Optionen- und Futures-Transaktionen. Koordination und Verbuchung von Transaktionen von nicht-deponierten Werten wie z.B. Private Equity, Infrastrukturanlagen, Immobilien etc.

• Mithilfe bei der Erstellung und Qualitätskontrolle von monatlichen und quartalsweisen kundenspezifischen Investmentreports (v.a. Fonds-Factsheets) für traditionelle und alternative Anlagen

Anforderungsprofil:

• Banklehre mit Berufsmaturität und abgeschlossene Weiterbildung (Bankwirtschafter/in HF, CFA /AZEK) oder Hochschulabschluss

• Mindestens fünf Jahre Berufserfahrung im Finanzsektor, idealerweise in der Betreuung von institutionellen Kunden

• Breite Fachkenntnisse im Bereich Asset Servicing (z. B. im Bereich Wertschriften, Börse, Back-& Middle-Office, Anlageberatung). Erfahrung im Kontakt mit externen Vermögensverwaltern und / oder Global Custody / Fonds-Kunden von Vorteil.

• Interesse am Fondsgeschäft, an operativen Abläufen (Prozessen) und Investmentthemen, verbunden mit Freude an Zahlen und regelmässigem Kontakt zu internen und externen Stellen

• Sehr gute MS Office Anwenderkenntnisse

• Kunden- und bedürfnisorientiert, hohes Qualitätsbewusstsein, genaue und selbstständige Arbeitsweise

• Teamfähige, engagierte und kommunikative Persönlichkeit mit hoher Soz

jobs.title: Equities & Derivatives Execution Trader (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

With over 250 professionals, our Investment Solutions department is responsible for providing sophisticated and highly customized investment proposals to our private clients.

Equities & Derivatives Execution Trader (80-100%)

Mission:

• Equities & Derivatives Trade Execution: Execute, monitor, and optimize the bank’s equity and derivative client flows across global markets, ensuring consistent best execution in line with applicable regulatory requirements and internal governance frameworks. The ideal candidate will have a strong expertise in future and option strategies).

• Late Shift: Take part in cross asset execution coverage until the U.S. market close (each execution trader participating in a limited number of late shifts per month)

• Technology & Automation: Actively Contribute to the enhancement of electronic trading strategies, leveraging trading data and analytics to help design implement, and scale new automation process

• Counterparty Interaction: Maintain strong relationships with brokers and market makers to optimize execution and access liquidity

Your Profile:

• Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Economics, or a related field

• Experience: Minimum of 3 years in derivatives and equity trading

• Technical Skills: Proficiency in Bloomberg, TradeWeb and order management systems

• Familiarity with algorithmic and electronic trading solutions is highly advantageous

• Analytical & Execution Skills: Strong market analysis capabilities, sound judgment under pressure, and ability to handle high trade volumes with precision and efficiency

• Communication : Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to communicate clearly with senior management, counterparties, and technology teams

• Teamwork: Team-oriented mindset with the ability to collaborate effectively with investment professionals and clients

• Project skills: Proactive and detail-oriented, with a strong sense of initiative and active participation in cross-functional projects

• Languages: Fluent in both English and French

• Service minded, accurate, autonomous

• Must be a resident of Switzerland or willing to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It

jobs.title: Business Risk Manager - Technology & Operations (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Risk Manager - Technology & Operations (80-100%)

As part of the Technology & Operations (T&O) Business Risk Management team, you will partner with stakeholders across Operations and Technology to identify key risks, coordinate remediation, and strengthen first‑line ownership of controls. You will contribute to the effective oversight of operational, technology and information risks, and ensure transparent and timely reporting to management.

YOUR ROLE

Risk Identification, Assessment & Reporting

• Identify, assess, and report operational risks, including processing, trade order execution, operational taxes, technology, cybersecurity, and information‑related risks across T&O.

• Support the RCSA process and ensure risk assessments remain current, well‑documented, and evidence‑based.

• Manage the T&O incident process, ensuring incidents are recorded, analysed, escalated, and followed through to closure.

Control Environment Oversight

• Contribute to the T&O Risk Assurance Plan through thematic reviews, independent design and operating effectiveness testing of the control environment, and follow‑up of identified gaps.

• Support the ISAE control framework, ensuring required evidence is collected, complete, and audit‑ready.

Remediation & Issue Management

• Drive, coordinate, and track risk remediation activities, ensuring actions are well defined, owned, and delivered on time.

• Monitor and report on the progression of identified issues, ensuring consistent closure, documentation, and controls uplift.

Governance, Assurance & Culture

• Promote a strong risk culture by supporting awareness, training, and constructive challenge across teams.

• Prepare structured and insightful risk reports to the T&O Risk Committee and senior management.

YOUR PROFILE

• Minimum 5 years' experience in risk management, internal control, audit, or consulting (e.g., Big Four) with focus on operational or technology‑related risks.

• Solid understanding of operations, IT, cybersecurity, and information risk, ideally within financial services.

• Strong analytical mindset with a structured, detail‑oriented approach.

• Excellent communication skills in English and French, with the ability to challenge stakeholders constructively.

• Resident in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

• Resident in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe
descriptionByLocale.it:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Risk Manager - Technology & Operations (80-100%)

As part of the Technology & Operations (T&O) Business Risk Management team, you will partner with stakeholders across Operations and Technology to identify key risks, coordinate remediation, and strengthen first‑line ownership of controls. You will contribute to the effective oversight of operational, technology and information risks, and ensure transparent and timely reporting to management.

YOUR ROLE

Risk Identification, Assessment & Reporting

• Identify, assess, and report operational risks, including processing, trade order execution, operational taxes, technology, cybersecurity, and information‑related risks across T&O.

• Support the RCSA process and ensure risk assessments remain current, well‑documented, and evidence‑based.

• Manage the T&O incident process, ensuring incidents are recorded, analysed, escalated, and followed through to closure.

Control Environment Oversight

• Contribute to the T&O Risk Assurance Plan through thematic reviews, independent design and operating effectiveness testing of the control environment, and follow‑up of identified gaps.

• Support the ISAE control framework, ensuring required evidence is collected, complete, and audit‑ready.

Remediation & Issue Management

• Drive, coordinate, and track risk remediation activities, ensuring actions are well defined, owned, and delivered on time.

• Monitor and report on the progression of identified issues, ensuring consistent closure, documentation, and controls uplift.

Governance, Assurance & Culture

• Promote a strong risk culture by supporting awareness, training, and constructive challenge across teams.

• Prepare structured and insightful risk reports to the T&O Risk Committee and senior management.

YOUR PROFILE

• Minimum 5 years' experience in risk management, internal control, audit, or consulting (e.g., Big Four) with focus on operational or technology‑related risks.

• Solid understanding of operations, IT, cybersecurity, and information risk, ideally within financial services.

• Strong analytical mindset with a structured, detail‑oriented approach.

• Excellent communication skills in English and French, with the ability to challenge stakeholders constructively.

• Resident in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe

titleByLocale.en: Stagiaire - Database Administration & Observability Specialist (Foglight / Elastic)

titleByLocale.de: Stagiaire - Database Administration & Observability Spezialist (Foglight / Elastic)

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Stagiaire - Database Administration & Observability Specialist (Foglight / Elastic)

Dans le cadre du renforcement de la supervision des bases de données et du déploiement d’une plateforme d’observabilité centralisée, l’équipe DBA recherche un(e) stagiaire pour accompagner un projet stratégique autour de Foglight (monitoring bases de données) et de son intégration avec la plateforme Elastic utilisée à l’échelle de la banque.

Ce stage offre une exposition concrète à des environnements bancaires critiques , des technologies de supervision modernes et à un projet transverse impliquant plusieurs équipes IT (DBA, Systèmes, Sécurité, Observabilité).

Le/la stagiaire accompagnera l’équipe DBA dans le suivi, la coordination, le déploiement et la documentation du projet Foglight, ainsi que dans son intégration avec la plateforme d’observabilité Elastic .

Le stage combine :

• Pilotage fonctionnel de projet,

• Mise en œuvre technique encadrée,

• Communication et documentation,

• Découverte des bonnes pratiques de supervision en milieu bancaire.

VOTRE MISSION

1. Pilotage et coordination du projet Foglight

• Participer au suivi du planning global Foglight & Elastic .

• Organiser et coordonner les réunions avec :

• Les référents des différentes technologies DB au sein de l’équipe DBA,

• Les équipes Systèmes, Sécurité et Observabilité.

• Préparer les comptes-rendus , tableaux de suivi et reportings d’avancement.

• Contribuer à la circulation fluide de l’information entre les équipes.

2. Implémentation de la supervision Foglight

Accompagné(e) par les experts DBA, le/la stagiaire participera à :

• L’ajout des environnements Test / UAT / Production dans Foglight.

• La validation des métriques collectées pour les différents SGBD.

• La création de dashboards de santé haut niveau par technologie.

• La définition et le paramétrage des alertes pertinentes .

• L’intégration avec ServiceNow pour l’ouverture automatique d’incidents.

• L’intégration avec Elastic pour la remontée des métriques de supervision.

3. Documentation et structuration

• Rédiger et maintenir la documentation projet .

• Produire la documentation fonctionnelle de l’intégration Foglight → Elastic .

• Créer des guides utilisateurs et procédures internes destinés aux équipes IT.

4. Support à la formation interne

• Préparer des supports de formation (présentations, guides).

• Contribuer à l’organisation et au bon déroulement de sessions de formation internes sur Foglight et Elastic.

5. Communication et visibilité du projet

• Préparer les communications internes officielles liées au projet.

• Mettre à jour les espaces Confluence / Teams .

• Participer à la communication autour des sujets d’ observabilité au sein de la banque.

Missions spécifiques – Observabilité Elastic

• Recueillir et formaliser les besoins en métriques, alertes et dashboards Elastic.

• Décrire fonctionnellement les flux de données Foglight → Elastic .

• Coordonner les échanges entre l’équipe DBA et l’équipe O

• Créer des guides utilisateurs et procédures internes destinés aux équipes IT.

4. Support à la formation interne

• Préparer des supports de formation (présentations, guides).

• Contribuer à l’organisation et au bon déroulement de sessions de formation internes sur Foglight et Elastic.

5. Communication et visibilité du projet

• Préparer les communications internes officielles liées au projet.

• Mettre à jour les espaces Confluence / Teams .

• Participer à la communication autour des sujets d’ observabilité au sein de la banque.

Missions spécifiques – Observabilité Elastic

• Recueillir et formaliser les besoins en métriques, alertes et dashboards Elastic.

• Décrire fonctionnellement les flux de données Foglight → Elastic .

• Coordonner les échanges entre l’équipe DBA et l’équipe O

jobs.title: IT Security Engineer (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

IT Security Engineer (80-100%)

The Security Engineer designs, implements and manages security solutions, ensuring an effective defense against cyber threats or data theft. Responsibilities include threat detection, vulnerability analysis, handling support tickets, and implementing security controls and related solutions (data leakage prevention, vulnerability management, security hardening, public key infrastructure, secrets management, privileged access management and endpoint security). The security engineer will also educate staff on best practices.

YOUR ROLE

• Design, implement, and maintain security solutions

• Conduct and/or contribute to security projects

• Assess and improve the security hardening of enterprise solutions

• Participate in alert and incident response reviews and propose improvement actions

• Propose new security solutions and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives

• Develop security workflows and automate recurring tasks

• Maintain comprehensive documentation of system configurations, procedures, incident reports, security requirements

• Contribute to enforcing robust security tools and secure coding practices in development

YOUR PROFILE

• Bachelor’s degree computer engineering, security engineering or a relevant field. Master degree is an engineering school is a plus

• Sound experience of Data Loss Prevention (DLP) solutions and best practices

• Strong interest in IT security, continuous learning, secure engineering practices and threat modeling, IT market trends

• Strong knowledge of Azure cloud, Microsoft 365 services and hybrid cloud services

• Excellent command of automation, scripting, Infrastructure‑as‑Code (IaC) principles, common software architecture patterns, networking protocols and technologies

• Highly analytical and rigorous, with a solution‑oriented mindset and strong autonomy

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to communicate with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders

• Highly versatile and able to adapt to diverse technical contexts

• Fluency in English and French

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Business Manager – Platform Delivery & Client Engagement (80–100%)

Lombard Odier provides its proprietary private‑banking platform to a growing number of external institutions via an ITO/BPO model. Banking Platform Services (BPS) acts as the central interface with these Partner Banks, coordinating platform delivery, change management, and service operations.
To strengthen our operating model, we are looking for a Business Manager dedicated to enhancing our processes, standardizing client‑engagement practices, and implementing the tools that allow us to manage the change roadmap, service cases, and governance frameworks effectively.

YOUR ROLE

• Define, standardize, and continuously improve processes across BPS (change management, case management, governance, reporting).

• Implement and maintain best‑in‑class tooling for client‑relationship workflows, including roadmap oversight, demand intake, service‑management cases, KPIs, and dashboards.

• Work closely with Client Platform Advisors and Relationship Managers to ensure consistent client experience and delivery quality across all Partner Banks.

• Coordinate cross‑functional initiatives with IT, Product, Operations, and Risk to streamline how client demands are captured, assessed, and executed.

• Lead internal projects aimed at increasing operational efficiency, transparency, and scalability.

• Drive documentation standards and support internal knowledge‑management practices.

• Monitor platform evolution and ensure all client‑facing processes remain aligned with strategic priorities.

YOUR PROFILE

• University degree in business, technology, operations, or a related field.

• 5+ years’ experience in consulting, business- or project management in financial services or technology.

• Strong understanding of operating models, process optimisation, and tooling implementation.

• Experience with service‑management or project‑management solutions (ServiceNow, Jira, Confluence, PowerBI, etc.).

• Excellent organisational, analytical, and communication skills.

• Ability to work with multiple stakeholders and drive change across teams.

• Fluency in French and English.

• Resident in Switzerland or willingness to relocate.

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embe

jobs.title: Junior Business Analyst - Client Platform Advisory (80-100%)

titleByLocale.en: Junior Business Analyst - Client Platform Advisory (80-100%)
titleByLocale.it: Junior Business Analista - Client Platform Advisory (80-100%)
titleByLocale.de: Junior Business Analyst - Client Platform Advisory (80-100%)
titleByLocale.fr: Junior Business Analyste - Client Platform Advisory (80-100%)

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Junior Business Analyst - Client Platform Advisory (80-100%)

Lombard Odier has developed a proprietary, fully integrated private‑banking platform used by the Group and by external financial institutions through a BPO/ITO delivery model. Our Technology & Operations division builds, operates, and continually evolves this platform.

We are looking for a Junior Client Platform Advisor to join our Banking Platform Services (BPS) team. In this role, you will support senior advisors in delivering high‑quality platform services to our Partner Banks, helping them make the most of our core‑banking technology (G2/GX). This position offers a unique opportunity to grow into a client‑facing advisory role while developing strong expertise in private‑banking processes and technology.

YOUR ROLE

• Support senior advisors in managing day‑to‑day interactions with Partner Banks and understanding their platform needs.

• Assist in gathering, analyzing, and prioritizing client requests across multiple functional domains.

• Contribute to translating business needs into clear requirements for IT and Operations teams.

• Help track change requests, roadmap items, and platform enhancements.

• Develop familiarity with LO’s proprietary private‑banking platform and its front‑to‑back architecture.

• Participate in workshops, testing activities, and internal coordination meetings.

• Stay informed about platform evolutions and industry trends in wealth‑management technology.

YOUR PROFILE

• University degree in business, IT, finance, or a related field.

• 1–3 years’ experience in banking, consulting, business analysis, or technology (internships included).

• Familiarity with project‑management or collaboration tools (e.g., Jira, Confluence, MS Office) is a plus.

• Strong interest in private banking, digital platforms, and process optimization.

• Analytical mindset with the ability to synthesize complex topics.

• Team player with strong communication skills and willingness to learn.

• Fluency in French and English.

• Resident in Switzerland or willingness to relocate.

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be ha

jobs.title: Operations Support Specialist - Houston team (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Operations Support Specialist - Houston team (80-100%)

Become a key member of our dynamic nine-person team within the Operations department and become the central point of contact for a large scope of teams and actively support our business. As a central interface at T&O Operations for a wide range of internal clients in front teams or other departments in Geneva, Luxembourg, and Singapore, you will ensure smooth and effective operations.

YOUR ROLE

• Communication Hub: Act as the single point of contact for internal queries related to securities transfers, settlements, funds, custody, cash services, tax reporting, and precious metals. Resolve issues independently with a solution-oriented mindset.

• Risk Awareness : Maintain high vigilance and risk awareness to identify, assess, and prioritize operational risks effectively.

• Innovation : Develop creative solutions that meet client expectations while adhering to internal policies, banking regulations, and legal requirements.

• Client focus : Provide regular reports on operational performance, identify trends, and suggest improvements. Organize feedback sessions with internal clients.

• Collaboration : Support internal teams and operations in managing projects, monitoring exceptions, and delivering constructive feedback.

• Operational Excellence : Build broad expertise across banking operations (e.g., corporate actions, funds, taxes, invoicing, settlements, payments) and contribute to a knowledge hub for middle office functions.

YOUR PROFILE

• Completed banking apprenticeship; ideally supplemented by a completed or ongoing degree in business administration or business information technology

• Several years of experience in banking Middle and Back Office activities

• Good command of securities operations

• Excellent interpersonal skills, client-oriented mindset, strong attention to details, good stakeholders’ management skills

• Ability to manage deadlines in a dynamic, multidisciplinary, and transversal environment

• Proficient in French and English, German proficiency will be appreciated

• Resident in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

• Several years of experience in banking Middle and Back Office activities

• Good command of securities operations

• Excellent interpersonal skills, client-oriented mindset, strong attention to details, good stakeholders’ management skills

• Ability to manage deadlines in a dynamic, multidisciplinary, and transversal environment

• Proficient in French and English, German proficiency will be appreciated

• Resident in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

jobs.title: Corporate Action Specialist (80-100%) (12-month contract)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Corporate Action Specialist (80-100%) (12-month contract)

Join our dynamic Corporate Actions team of 12 collaborators and play a key role in managing corporate actions and securities operations on shares, bonds and other financial products (warrants, rights …).
This is an exciting opportunity to contribute to a fast-paced environment and collaborate with both internal stakeholders and leading external partners.

YOUR ROLE

• Process corporate actions on equities, bonds, and structured products, from analysis to booking and reconciliation.

• Handle both mandatory and voluntary events such as capital increases, tender offers, mergers, and stock splits.

• Identify and reconcile eligible holders.

• Generate and distribute notifications related to corporate actions announcements.

• Manage clients instructions related to elective corporate actions.

• Book transactions upon receipt of confirmations within the required deadlines.

• Resolve position breaks.

• Escalate complex cases to ensure prompt resolution.

• Act as a point of contact for our clients, portfolio managers, financial analysts, traders, and our network of custodian banks.

• Update procedures and contribute to administrative tasks within the department.

• Support requests from the Legal / Compliance department or audit-related inquiries.

• Identify potential issues related to operational workflows.

• Propose continuous improvements to our IT applications.

YOUR PROFILE

• Experience of 2-3 years in securities operations (Back Office).

• Experience in Corporate Actions and/or Proxy Voting is an asset.

• Autonomous, strong sense of priorities, team spirit.

• Ability to adapt in a complex environment and manage stress effectively.

• Fluent in French and English (spoken and written).

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.title: HR Finance Specialist (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

HR Finance Specialist (80-100%)

HR Finance is handling all processes related to people costs budgeting and forecasting to support the goals of the organization while ensuring a balance between revenue and costs to drive sustainable growth.

YOUR ROLE

• Manage the budgeting and forecasting process for the Unit and ensure precise HR costs monitoring as well as other aspects of HR Finance, and liaison with Group HR Finance.

• Perform payroll reconciliation processes between SAP and Workday and manages all payroll provisions to ensure accuracy and compliance.

• Manage the headcount plan, maintain and follow up on open positions and people cost, explain monthly variances (joiners, leavers, transfers), analyze trends and associated cost analysis.

• Responsible for regular HR reporting and analysis primarily in relation to headcount, people cost, Diversity, recruitment, and other HR KPI’s etc.

• Additional support to the HR team including other HR Projects, payroll, and other employee related queries.

• An active role in the compensation review cycle as well as salary benchmarking.

YOUR PROFILE

• University degree in Finance, Economics or in fields related to Business Administration.

• You have a minimum of 3-5 years’ experience in related HR Finance, Business Management, HR analytical role or Audit..

• Proven expertise in managing and processing confidential data, automating workflow processes, and overseeing database systems.

• An advanced knowledge and technical ability with MS Excel (VBA, SQL, advanced Excel a must) & and BI tools and systems (SAP, Tableau, etc.).

• A solid understanding of payroll and accounting processes is an asset.

• An ability to analyze and present data concisely.

• Project management experience would be a plus.

• Excellent problem-solving skills in constructive/realizable solution.

• Languages: French and English (both written and spoken).

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

IT Production Engineer – Scheduling / File Transfer / Monitoring (80-100%)

As part of the modernization of our IT platform and the adoption of new technologies, we are looking for a junior production engineer eager to learn and grow in areas such as Cloud, DevOps, and AI-driven automation. You will join a dynamic team of 7 engineers within the IT Operations department and contribute to the evolution of our production environment. The team is responsible for Scheduling, File Transfer and Monitoring inside IT Delivery Team

YOUR ROLE

• Operate and monitor production systems in a hybrid environment (on-premise and Cloud).

• Implement and automate workflows using DevOps practices (CI/CD pipelines, scripting).

• Participate in Cloud migration projects and help define best practices.

• Contribute to AI-based initiatives for predictive monitoring and process optimization.

• Provide Level 2/3 support for production incidents and collaborate with technical teams.

• Document processes and propose improvements for reliability and efficiency.

• Internal Contacts: Production team (Product-Support, Exploitation), Integration team, Infrastructure teams (Databases, Systems, Networks), Development teams, Service Management teams (Incidents and Problems)

• External Contacts: Cloud service providers, Technology partners, and vendors

YOUR PROFILE

• Degree in Computer Science or equivalent.

• Zero to 3-4 years of experience in a similar role

• Knowledge of Cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, or GCP).

• Knowledge of DevOps tools (Git, Jenkins/GitLab CI, Docker, Kubernetes).

• Knowledge of scripting (Python, Bash) and interest in automation.

• Curiosity about AI technologies and their application in IT operations.

• Good analytical skills, willingness to learn, and team spirit.

• Fluent in French and English (spoken and written).

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate.

TECHNICAL ENVIRONMENT

• Cloud: Azure

• DevOps: Git, AzureDevOps CI/CD, Jenkins, Docker, Kubernetes

• Automation & Scripting: Opcon, GoAnyhere, Python, Bash, PowerShell

• Monitoring & Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, ServiceNow Event Management

• AI & Analytics: APIs for predictive monitoring, anomaly detection tools

• Infrastructure as Code: Terraform, Ansible

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contri

jobs.title: Integration and DevOps Engineer (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Integration and DevOps Engineer (80-100%)

Within the IT Operations unit, our team (16 people between Luxembourg and Geneva) is dedicated to integrate new business applications into our ecosystem according to the requirements of different stakeholders (Security/Platform/Production/Infrastructure and Dev). Our team makes our core app evolution easier and smoother, without compromising quality.

YOUR ROLE

• Manage the release management process to reduce operational risk in prod and test environments:

• Coordinate planned changes between dev and production teams

• Facilitate the Change Advisory Board (CAB) meetings

• Efficiently integrate new business applications into the ecosystem from design to implementation in various environments (Dev/Test/UAT and prod)

• Coordinate and execute technical tasks based on the requirements of different stakeholders (Security/Platform/Production/Infrastructure/provider and Dev)

• Identify and mitigate risks, technical issues, and dependencies across the project

• Collaborate with the Provider (Third-party Package) on Lombard Odier technical requirements

• Develop the project's reference document in collaboration with Dev

• Estimate the workload of IT tasks during the project

• Implement continuous improvement using the Problem Management process to reduce downtime for business applications- Internal stakeholders: Development, Service Management (Incident/Problem Management), DBA, Network, Citrix/Virtualization, Tooling, IT Security, IT Risk.

• External stakeholders: Software and Service Vendors, Business Process Outsourcing Clients (Banks who are using our IT Platform and services)

YOUR PROFILE

• Master in IT or equivalent

• Good command of development languages and linked application servers: JAVA, JBOSS, C++

• Good command of CI/CD tooling: Openshift, Kubernetes, DevOps principles

• Good command of production tooling: FTP transferts, schedulers

• Previous experience of the Banking sector will be an asset

• Responsible, rigorous, versatile, with good interpersonal skills and team spirit. Ready to propose enhancements and participate to continuous improvment initiatives.

• Fluent in English and French

TECHNICAL ENVIRONMENT

• Service Now

• Serena

• ArgoCd

• NetScaler

• Java

• Apache/IIS

• Openshift

• Kubernetes

NB: Please be aware that in accordance with the current law and job requirements, the selected candidate will be requested to provide a criminal record (section 3).

descriptionByLocale.fr:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Integration and DevOps Engineer (80-100%)

Within the IT Operations unit, our team (16 people between Luxembourg and Geneva) is dedicated to integrate new business applications into our ecosystem according to the requirements of different stakeholders (Security/Platform/Production/Infrastructure and Dev). Our team makes our core app evolution easier and smoother, without compromising quality.

YOUR ROLE

• Manage the release management process to reduce operational risk in prod and test environments:

• Coordinate planned changes between dev and production teams

• Facilitate the Change Advisory Board (CAB) meetings

• Efficiently integrate new business applications into the ecosystem from design to implementation in various environments (Dev/Test/UAT and prod)

• Coordinate and execute technical tasks based on the requirements of different stakeholders (Security/Platform/Production/Infrastructure/provider and Dev)

• Identify and mitigate risks, technical issues, and dependencies across the project

• Collaborate with the Provider (Third-party Package) on Lombard Odier technical requirements

• Develop the project's reference document in collaboration with Dev

• Estimate the workload of IT tasks during the project

• Implement continuous improvement using the Problem Management process to reduce downtime for business applications- Internal stakeholders: Development, Service Management (Incident/Problem Management), DBA, Network, Citrix/Virtualization, Tooling, IT Security, IT Risk.

• External stakeholders: Software and Service Vendors, Business Process Outsourcing Clients (Banks who are using our IT Platform and services)

YOUR PROFILE

• Master in IT or equivalent

• Good command of development languages and linked application servers: JAVA, JBOSS, C++

• Good command of CI/CD tooling: Openshift, Kubernetes, DevOps principles

• Good command of production tooling: FTP transferts, schedulers

• Previous experience of the Banking sector will be an asset

• Responsible, rigorous, versatile, with good interpersonal skills and team spirit. Ready to propose enhancements and participate to continuous improvment initiatives.

• Fluent in English and French

TECHNICAL ENVIRONMENT

• Service Now

• Serena

• ArgoCd

• NetScaler

• Java

• Apache/IIS

• Openshift

• Kubernetes

NB: Please be aware that in accordance with the current law and job requirements, the selected candidate will be requested to provide a criminal record (section 3).

jobs.title: M&A Intern - 6 months
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Part of the Lombard Odier Group, Lombard Odier Corporate Advisory supports business owners in their strategic M&A decisions, succession planning and family governance.

M&A Intern - 6 months - start date 1st of August or 1st of September

Your Role :

• Support the Corporate Advisory team on M&A and corporate finance mandates across sell-side, buy-side and valuation engagements

• Participate in a broad range of tasks including company and market research, preparation of proposals/pitches and transaction documents (teasers, information memoranda, financial analyses, etc.)

• Assist in the preparation of financial models and valuation materials under the supervision of Analyst and Associate

• Contribute to the commercial effort of the team through industry research and preparation of meeting materials

• Ensure rigorous follow-up of internal workstreams and deliverables with proactivity and strong attention to detail

• Liaise with internal partners and departments to support the delivery of timely and accurate information for clients and prospects

Your Profile :

• University student enrolled in a relevant Master’s or Bachelor’s degree program

• Person willing to work in an agile and collaborative environment

• Strong numerical and analytical capabilities, including understanding of financial statements analysis, valuation and modelling concepts

• Excellent writing, communication and presentation skills

• Very good team spirit, reliability and sense of client service

• Diligent, autonomous and able to multitask effectively

• Very good IT skills, particularly in Excel and PowerPoint

• Strong sense of confidentiality

• Very good level of English and French. German and/or Swiss German is an asset

• First experience in investment banking, corporate finance, transaction advisory, private equity, management consulting or similar environment is an asset

• Residence in Switzerland

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting time to join our Teams. All applications will be handled in the strictest confidence.

jobs.title: Settlement Specialist (12-month contract) (80–100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Settlement Specialist (12-month contract) (80–100%)

The Settlement Specialist is responsible for ensuring the successful and timely settlement of transactions. They monitor and reconcile failing trades.
A strong understanding of trading operations and excellent communication skills are essential for effective operational management.

YOUR ROLE

• Daily monitoring of pending transactions through to final settlement.

• Analysis and management of complex transactions and exceptional situations.

• Identification of recurring changes in settlement instructions; request corrections/creation of networks (SSI) to maximize automation.

• Propose solutions to increase operational efficiency and optimize processes.

• Coordinate and verify incoming/outgoing payments for exceptions (e.g., netting).

• Daily collaboration and communication with clients, custodians, and internal teams to resolve settlement issues.

• Perform daily reconciliation of cash and securities positions, resolve breaks, and ensure proper traceability with consistent commentary.

• Ensure compliance with financial regulations and internal policies regarding Delivery versus Payment (DvP) and Free of Payment (FoP/Lending).

• Adhere to regulatory requirements and company/group policies.

• Identify and mitigate operational risks and review procedures.

YOUR PROFILE

• Bachelor’s degree in finance or equivalent in business administration. Swiss banking apprenticeship (CFC Banque) / Professional Banking Maturity also considered.

• Strong knowledge of SWIFT ISO 20022 (particularly MT54x messages), regulatory requirements, and custodian platforms.

• Good knowledge of financial instruments (bonds, equities, etc.).

• Experience in other back- or middle-office functions is a plus.

• Strong team spirit and ability to build solid professional relationships.

• Ability to work under pressure, meet tight deadlines, and manage priorities effectively.

• Ability to challenge existing processes and propose improvements.

• Fluency in French and English (minimum B2 level); German is an asset.

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate.

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

descriptionByLocale.fr:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Settlement Specialist (12-month contract) (80–100%)

The Settlement Specialist is responsible for ensuring the successful and timely settlement of transactions. They monitor and reconcile failing trades.
A strong understanding of trading operations and excellent communication skills are essential for effective operational management.

YOUR ROLE

• Daily monitoring of pending transactions through to final settlement.

• Analysis and management of complex transactions and exceptional situations.

• Identification of recurring changes in settlement instructions; request corrections/creation of networks (SSI) to maximize automation.

• Propose solutions to increase operational efficiency and optimize processes.

• Coordinate and verify incoming/outgoing payments for exceptions (e.g., netting).

• Daily collaboration and communication with clients, custodians, and internal teams to resolve settlement issues.

• Perform daily reconciliation of cash and securities positions, resolve breaks, and ensure proper traceability with consistent commentary.

• Ensure compliance with financial regulations and internal policies regarding Delivery versus Payment (DvP) and Free of Payment (FoP/Lending).

• Adhere to regulatory requirements and company/group policies.

• Identify and mitigate operational risks and review procedures.

YOUR PROFILE

• Bachelor’s degree in finance or equivalent in business administration. Swiss banking apprenticeship (CFC Banque) / Professional Banking Maturity also considered.

• Strong knowledge of SWIFT ISO 20022 (particularly MT54x messages), regulatory requirements, and custodian platforms.

• Good knowledge of financial instruments (bonds, equities, etc.).

• Experience in other back- or middle-office functions is a plus.

• Strong team spirit and ability to build solid professional relationships.

• Ability to work under pressure, meet tight deadlines, and manage priorities effectively.

• Ability to challenge existing processes and propose improvements.

• Fluency in French and English (minimum B2 level); German is an asset.

• Residence in Switzerland or willingness to relocate.

Our Maison’s DNA is defined by five core values. Excellence drives us to be the best at what we do, while Innovation fuels our progress. Respect underpins every interaction, and Integrity shapes our actions. Together, we are One Team , united in serving our clients with unwavering dedication.

As a responsible and supportive employer, we promote a diverse and inclusive work environment for our employees and candidates. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion are woven into the fabric of our Maison’s DNA, and we strive to ensure that our employees can fulfill both their personal and professional aspirations by encouraging internal mobility and individual upskilling programs. We firmly believe that building Diverse Teams contributes to our successes and to deliver on this, we actively embed Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in our business strategy.

It is an exciting ti

jobs.title: SAP FI Application Expert (80-100%)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

SAP FI Application Expert (80-100%)

Within our IT Department, you will join a team of experts responsible for the evolution of our financial ecosystem. In a Private Banking environment where data precision and availability are critical, you will play a key role within a scope covering accounting, business intelligence, planning and consolidation, and expense management. Your mission will be threefold:

• Ensure operational continuity through second and third-level support,

• Steer the evolution of the platform in close collaboration with the SAP product owner, and Finance stakeholders,

• Contribute to cross-functional project delivery.

You will also participate to our technological transition toward next-generation solutions (S/4HANA).

YOUR ROLE

Product Evolution & Project Delivery:

• Gather and analyze business requirements from Finance and Controlling teams.

• Design functional solutions and write specifications

• Contribute to BPC planning model evolution and BW/4, SAP Analytics Cloud reporting developments

• S/4HANA Transformation: Actively contribute to S/4 Hana conversion projects and new onboarding projects.

• Manage end-to-end delivery of change requests and projects (scoping, testing, training, go-live).

• Configure SAP FI (GL, AR, AP, Asset Accounting) and CO modules.

• Ensure all changes comply with internal change management and audit frameworks.

Application support & maintenance:

• Provide Level 2 / Level 3 functional and technical support for SAP FI/CO, BW/4 Hana, SAC, BPC and Concur.

• Investigate, diagnose and resolve incidents within agreed SLAs; escalate to SAP when required.

• Monitor batch jobs, interfaces and data flows; ensure system health and data integrity.

• Manage transports, patches and corrections across the landscape.

• Maintain functional and technical documentation up to date after every change.

YOUR PROFILE

• Minimum 7 years of hands-on SAP FI (80%) / CO (20%) experience in a complex, regulated environment (functional & technical expertise)

• Proven track record in both support/run activities and project mode

• Good command of SAP BW reporting, BPC planning & consolidation, and SAP Concur configuration and integration with SAP FI

• Knowledge or experience on S/4HANA architecture and migration concepts will be appreciated

• Solid understanding of financial accounting processes: general ledger, accounts payable/receivable, asset management, bank reconciliation

• Familiarity with private banking operational flows (client accounting, fees, treasury, regulatory reporting) is a plus

• Strong analytical mindset with ability to translate complex functional requirements into technical solutions

• Excellent communication skills – able to present to both technical and business audiences

• Rigorous, autonomous and structured; comfortable managing several tasks and priorities simultaneously

• Collaborative team player with a service-oriented attitude

• Full proficiency in French and English

• Residence

jobs.title: Systems Engineer – Automation (Windows/VMware)
jobs.description:
A career at Lombard Odier means working for a renowned global wealth and asset manager, with a strong focus on sustainable investing. An innovative bank of choice for private and institutional clients, our independently owned Firm is one of the best-capitalised banking groups in the world, managing close to CHF 300 billion and operating from over 25 offices across 4 continents.

With a history spanning over 230 years, Lombard Odier is an investment house providing a comprehensive offering of discretionary and advisory portfolio management, wealth services and custody. We also offer asset management services and investment strategies through Lombard Odier Investment Managers and provide advanced banking technology to other financial institutions.

“Rethink Everything” is our philosophy – it is at the heart of everything we do. We have grown stronger through more than 40 financial crises by rethinking the world around us to provide a fresh investment perspective for our clients.

Systems Engineer – Automation (Windows/VMware)

We are looking for a Windows Systems Engineer with a strong passion for automation and infrastructure optimization. You will join a dynamic team of 15 system engineering experts who manage a global infrastructure of more than 7,000 servers. This is a large-scale technical environment where expertise, innovation and advanced automation are at the core of our mission.
You will play a key role in the evolution and standardization of our IT landscape, with a strong focus on PowerShell, VMware Aria/vRealize Automation, and Windows technologies such as Windows Server and Active Directory.
You will be involved in the design, implementation and maintenance of automation solutions aimed at improving operational efficiency and reducing manual intervention.
We actively encourage innovation and the proposal of new ideas to enhance our platforms.
We are currently driving several strategic initiatives, including the upgrade to VMware Cloud Foundation 9 (VCF 9) to modernize our virtualization stack, as well as a next generation observability project based on Elastic solutions.

YOUR ROLE

• Design, develop and maintain advanced PowerShell scripts to automate system administration tasks.

• Deploy, maintain, and optimize VMware Aria / vRealize Automation workflows and blueprints.

• Participate in the administration and evolution of Windows platforms, including Windows Server, Active Directory / ADFS / GPO, SCCM (Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager).

• Contribute to the adoption of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) practices.

• Ensure performance, security, and reliability across virtual and physical infrastructure.

• Collaborate closely with network, security, and development teams to integrate automation solutions across the organization.

• Support industrialization efforts, migrations, and continuous improvement projects.

YOUR PROFILE

• Excellent command of Windows Server administration.

• Excellent command of PowerShell (scripting, modules, APIs, end to end automation).

• Good command of VMware Aria / vRealize Automation (vRA, vRO, blueprints, workflows). Knowledge of other Aria suite components is considered an asset.

• Knowledge of virtualization technologies (VMware vSphere).

• Knowledge of Infrastructure as Code (IaC), CI/CD applied to automation, REST APIs, and system integrations

• Experience with Azure, Terraform, GIT and Ansible is considered an asset.

• Strong problem-solving mindset and continuous improvement orientation.

• Ability to work independently in a complex environment.

• Strong service orientation and excellent communication skills.

• Analytical approach and rigorous documentation practices.

NB: Please be aware that in accordance with the current law and job requirements, the selected candidate will be requested to provide a criminal record (section 3).
